functions.name: QObject
functions.description: Base class for most Qt object hierarchies.
functions.longDescription: QObject provides parent-child ownership, the meta-object system, signals and slots, and event delivery.

functions.name: Q_OBJECT
functions.description: Enable Qt meta-object features in a QObject-derived class.
functions.longDescription: Required for custom signals, slots, properties, and runtime type information generated by moc.

functions.name: signals
functions.description: Qt signal section in a class declaration.
functions.longDescription: Signals declare notifications emitted by a QObject-based class.

functions.name: slots
functions.description: Qt slot section in a class declaration.
functions.longDescription: Slots are callable directly or through signal-slot connections.

functions.name: emit
functions.description: Emit a Qt signal.
functions.longDescription: The emit keyword clarifies intent when notifying connected receivers.

functions.name: QObject parent-child ownership
functions.description: Use QObject parents for automatic child deletion.
functions.longDescription: QObject object trees provide deterministic lifetime management for QObject-derived objects. Do not combine QObject parent ownership with a second owning smart pointer unless the ownership model is explicit.

functions.name: object.deleteLater
functions.description: Schedule QObject destruction in the event loop that owns the object.
functions.longDescription:
Prefer deleteLater() when QObject destruction must happen safely through its event loop. This is especially relevant for worker objects associated with a QThread.

Previous workflow example retained from the original Qt pack:

```cpp
worker->deleteLater();
```

functions.name: QObject::connect signal to member slot
functions.signature: QMetaObject::Connection QObject::connect(const Sender *sender, PointerToSignal signal, const Receiver *receiver, PointerToMemberFunction method, Qt::ConnectionType type = Qt::AutoConnection);
functions.description: Create a compile-time checked connection from a signal to a member slot.
functions.longDescription:
Use typed pointers to member functions. AutoConnection is generally appropriate unless cross-thread delivery semantics must be explicit.

Previous workflow example retained from the original Qt pack:

```cpp
connect(sender, &SenderType::valueChanged,
        receiver, &ReceiverType::setValue);
```

options.label: Qt::AutoConnection
options.description: Let Qt choose direct or queued delivery according to the sender and receiver thread affinity.

options.label: Qt::DirectConnection
options.description: Invoke the slot immediately in the emitting thread.

options.label: Qt::QueuedConnection
options.description: Queue delivery to the receiver event loop and return immediately from the emitter.

options.label: Qt::BlockingQueuedConnection
options.description: Queue delivery and block the emitting thread until the receiver finishes. Do not use this between objects in the same thread.

options.label: Qt::UniqueConnection
options.description: Prevent creation of an additional equivalent member-function connection.

pickerConfig.subtitle: Delivery mode.

functions.name: QObject::connect queued cross-thread
functions.signature: QMetaObject::Connection QObject::connect(const Sender *sender, PointerToSignal signal, const Receiver *receiver, PointerToMemberFunction method, Qt::ConnectionType type);
functions.description: Force asynchronous signal delivery through the receiver event loop.
functions.longDescription:
Queued connections are appropriate when sender and receiver may live in different threads. Ensure argument types can be transported through the Qt meta-object system.

Previous workflow example retained from the original Qt pack:

```cpp
connect(worker, &Worker::measurementReady,
        this, &MainWindow::applyMeasurement,
        Qt::QueuedConnection);
```
functions.declaration: QMetaObject::Connection QObject::connect(const Sender *sender, PointerToSignal signal, const Receiver *receiver, PointerToMemberFunction method, Qt::ConnectionType type);
functions.returnDescription: Handle for the created connection.

functions.name: QObject::connect UniqueConnection
functions.signature: QMetaObject::Connection QObject::connect(const Sender *sender, PointerToSignal signal, const Receiver *receiver, PointerToMemberFunction method, Qt::ConnectionType type);
functions.description: Avoid creating the same member-function connection more than once.
functions.longDescription:
UniqueConnection is intended for duplicate prevention on member-function connections. It is not a general duplicate filter for arbitrary lambdas.

Previous workflow example retained from the original Qt pack:

```cpp
connect(device, &Device::stateChanged,
        this, &MainWindow::refreshState,
        Qt::UniqueConnection);
```

functions.name: timer.setTimerType
functions.description: Choose the accuracy and power-consumption trade-off for a QTimer.
functions.longDescription:
Precise timers target accuracy while coarse timers may reduce wakeups. Select the loosest mode that satisfies the application requirement.

Previous workflow example retained from the original Qt pack:

```cpp
timer_->setTimerType(Qt::PreciseTimer);    // Request millisecond accuracy.
// timer_->setTimerType(Qt::CoarseTimer);   // Default: approximately 5% margin.
// timer_->setTimerType(Qt::VeryCoarseTimer); // Whole-second granularity.
```

options.label: Qt::PreciseTimer
options.description: Request the most accurate Qt timer class and avoid early wake-ups.

options.label: Qt::CoarseTimer
options.description: Allow moderate timing coalescing to reduce wake-up overhead.

options.label: Qt::VeryCoarseTimer
options.description: Allow coarse timing suitable for low-frequency background work.

functions.name: QTimer zero interval incremental work
functions.description: Process one short item whenever the event loop becomes available.
functions.longDescription: A zero interval timer is useful for incremental work only when every callback returns quickly. Do not keep the GUI event loop continuously busy.

functions.name: QTimer thread affinity rule

functions.longDescription: Use QTimer only from its owning thread, which must have an event loop.

functions.name: statusBar.showMessage
functions.description: Show a non-blocking status-bar message.
functions.longDescription:
Set timeout to zero to keep the message until explicitly cleared.

Previous workflow example retained from the original Qt pack:

```cpp
statusBar()->showMessage(
    tr("Configuration saved"),
    3000);
```

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: Display duration in milliseconds; zero keeps it visible.

functions.name: widget.setToolTip
functions.description: Assign contextual help shown when hovering over a widget.
functions.longDescription:
Keep tooltips concise and use richer documentation for long explanations.

Previous workflow example retained from the original Qt pack:

```cpp
ui->scanButton->setToolTip(
    tr("Scan Bluetooth Low Energy advertisements for five seconds."));
```

functions.name: QToolTip::showText
functions.signature: static void QToolTip::showText(const QPoint &pos, const QString &text, QWidget *widget = nullptr, const QRect &rect = QRect(), int msecShowTime = -1);
functions.description: Show a tooltip at a global screen position.
functions.longDescription:
Use for dynamic hints associated with pointer location or a custom interaction.

Previous workflow example retained from the original Qt pack:

```cpp
QToolTip::showText(
    QCursor::pos(),
    tr("Voltage is outside the expected range"),
    this);
```
functions.declaration: static void QToolTip::showText(const QPoint &pos, const QString &text, QWidget *widget = nullptr, const QRect &rect = QRect(), int msecShowTime = -1);

functions.name: trayIcon.showMessage
functions.signature: void QSystemTrayIcon::showMessage(const QString &title, const QString &message, QSystemTrayIcon::MessageIcon icon = Information, int millisecondsTimeoutHint = 10000);
functions.description: Request a desktop notification through a system tray icon.
functions.longDescription:
Platform support and visual behavior depend on the desktop environment.

Previous workflow example retained from the original Qt pack:

```cpp
auto *trayIcon = new QSystemTrayIcon(
    QIcon(":/icons/app.png"),
    this);
trayIcon->show();
trayIcon->showMessage(
    tr("Device console"),
    tr("Acquisition completed."),
    QSystemTrayIcon::Information,
    4000);
```

options.label: QSystemTrayIcon::NoIcon
options.description: Use `QSystemTrayIcon::NoIcon` for `trayIcon.showMessage.icon`. This Qt value selects the no icon behavior.


 USB CDC / virtual COM : QSerialPort
 Bluetooth HID or USB HID: use a HID library or platform API
 Generic USB endpoints : use libusb, WinUSB, or the vendor SDK
 Qt itself does not provide a generic raw USB endpoint API.

*/

functions.name: pixmap.scaled
functions.signature: QPixmap QPixmap::scaled(const QSize &size, Qt::AspectRatioMode aspectRatioMode = Qt::IgnoreAspectRatio, Qt::TransformationMode transformMode = Qt::FastTransformation) const;
functions.description: Create a scaled copy of a pixmap.
functions.longDescription:
Select KeepAspectRatio for image viewers to avoid deformation. The retained workflow assigns the scaled pixmap to a QLabel.

Previous workflow example retained from the original Qt pack:

```cpp
QPixmap pixmap(imagePath);
ui->label->setPixmap(pixmap.scaled(ui->label->size(),
                                   Qt::KeepAspectRatio,
                                   Qt::SmoothTransformation));
```

options.label: Qt::IgnoreAspectRatio
options.description: Resize without preserving the original aspect ratio.

options.label: Qt::KeepAspectRatio
options.description: Resize while fitting inside the target bounds and preserving aspect ratio.

options.label: Qt::KeepAspectRatioByExpanding
options.description: Resize while covering the target bounds and preserving aspect ratio, potentially cropping overflow.

functions.name: QLibrary resolve typed C export
functions.description: Resolve an exported C symbol from a dynamically loaded DLL or shared object.
functions.longDescription: QLibrary::resolve() returns a raw symbol pointer. Convert it to a function-pointer type that exactly matches the exported ABI. A C-compatible exported function avoids C++ name mangling.

functions.name: QLibrary validate mandatory symbol table
functions.description: Resolve several mandatory exports and reject an incomplete DLL early.
functions.longDescription: Resolve the complete required API before starting work. This prevents partially initialized applications when one expected DLL symbol is missing or renamed.

functions.name: QLibrary::resolve
functions.signature: static QFunctionPointer QLibrary::resolve(const QString &fileName, const char *symbol);
functions.description: Resolve one C ABI export with the static convenience overload.
functions.declaration: static QFunctionPointer QLibrary::resolve(const QString &fileName, const char *symbol);

functions.name: Cross-platform shared-library file name
functions.description: Select a runtime shared-library name for Windows, Linux, and macOS.
functions.longDescription: QLibrary is cross-platform, but deployed file names conventionally differ between Windows DLLs, Linux shared objects, and macOS dynamic libraries.

functions.name: QLibrary reusable loader class skeleton
functions.description: Wrap QLibrary and resolved function pointers inside a small reusable loader class.
functions.longDescription: A dedicated loader class centralizes path resolution, exported prototypes, diagnostics, and API validation. Keep the QLibrary object alive while any resolved function pointer may still be used.

functions.name: Export C ABI for QLibrary resolve
functions.signature: Export C ABI for QLibrary resolve
functions.description: Export a stable runtime-loadable C function from a Windows DLL or Linux shared object.
functions.longDescription: QLibrary::resolve() works most predictably with a stable C ABI. On Windows export the symbol with __declspec(dllexport); on GCC/Clang builds, explicitly requesting default visibility is useful when hidden visibility is enabled.
functions.declaration: Export C ABI for QLibrary resolve

functions.name: library.unload
functions.description: Request unloading of a dynamic library.
functions.longDescription:
Unload only after every function pointer resolved from the library is no longer used. The retained note explains the lifecycle risk.

Previous workflow example retained from the original Qt pack:

```cpp
/*
 Keep the QLibrary object alive as long as resolved function pointers are used.
 After unload(), every resolved pointer must be considered invalid.
 unload() can fail if another QLibrary instance still references the same library.
 For process-wide driver DLLs, keeping the library loaded until shutdown is often safer.
*/
```

functions.name: pluginLoader.instance
functions.description: Instantiate and access the root QObject exported by one Qt plugin.

functions.name: QLibrary versus QPluginLoader decision
functions.description: Choose between raw exported functions and a Qt plugin object.
functions.longDescription: QLibrary provides explicit runtime symbol resolution. QPluginLoader is intended for Qt plugins and returns a QObject root instance.

functions.name: QPluginLoader interface skeleton

functions.name: CMake Qt Widgets project with runtime DLL
functions.signature: CMake Qt Widgets project with runtime DLL
functions.insertText:
cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.16)

project(BLE_Sniffeur VERSION 0.1 LANGUAGES CXX)

set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD 17)
set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD_REQUIRED ON)

find_package(Qt6 REQUIRED COMPONENTS Widgets)
qt_standard_project_setup()

qt_add_executable(BLE_Sniffeur
    main.cpp
    mainwindow.cpp
    mainwindow.h
    mainwindow.ui
)

target_sources(BLE_Sniffeur PRIVATE
    bledllloader.cpp
    bledllloader.h
    bledevice.h
)

target_link_libraries(BLE_Sniffeur PRIVATE Qt6::Widgets)

set(BLE_DLL_SOURCE "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/Scan_BLE_DLL.dll")
if(EXISTS "${BLE_DLL_SOURCE}")
    add_custom_command(TARGET BLE_Sniffeur POST_BUILD
        COMMAND ${CMAKE_COMMAND} -E copy_if_different
            "${BLE_DLL_SOURCE}"
            "$<TARGET_FILE_DIR:BLE_Sniffeur>/Scan_BLE_DLL.dll"
        COMMENT "Copy Scan_BLE_DLL.dll beside the executable"
    )
else()
    message(WARNING "Scan_BLE_DLL.dll is missing from the project root")
endif()

set_target_properties(BLE_Sniffeur PROPERTIES
    MACOSX_BUNDLE TRUE
    WIN32_EXECUTABLE TRUE
)

include(GNUInstallDirs)
install(TARGETS BLE_Sniffeur
    BUNDLE DESTINATION .
    LIBRARY DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR}
    RUNTIME DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_BINDIR}
)

functions.name: CMake copy runtime DLL beside executable
functions.signature: CMake copy runtime DLL beside executable
functions.insertText:
set(DRIVER_DLL "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/Scan_BLE_DLL.dll")

if(EXISTS "${DRIVER_DLL}")
    add_custom_command(TARGET MyQtApp POST_BUILD
        COMMAND ${CMAKE_COMMAND} -E copy_if_different
            "${DRIVER_DLL}"
            "$<TARGET_FILE_DIR:MyQtApp>/Scan_BLE_DLL.dll"
        COMMENT "Copy runtime DLL beside MyQtApp"
    )
else()
    message(WARNING "Missing runtime DLL: ${DRIVER_DLL}")
endif()
functions.description: Copy a dynamically loaded DLL after each successful application build.
functions.longDescription: Use POST_BUILD with $<TARGET_FILE_DIR:...> so the runtime DLL is copied next to the produced executable for the active build configuration.
functions.declaration: CMake copy runtime DLL beside executable

functions.name: CMake cross-platform sidecar shared library
functions.insertText:
if(WIN32)
    set(DRIVER_FILE "Scan_BLE_DLL.dll")
elseif(APPLE)
    set(DRIVER_FILE "libScan_BLE_DLL.dylib")
else()
    set(DRIVER_FILE "libScan_BLE_DLL.so")
endif()

set(DRIVER_SOURCE "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/${DRIVER_FILE}")
if(EXISTS "${DRIVER_SOURCE}")
    add_custom_command(TARGET MyQtApp POST_BUILD
        COMMAND ${CMAKE_COMMAND} -E copy_if_different
            "${DRIVER_SOURCE}"
            "$<TARGET_FILE_DIR:MyQtApp>/${DRIVER_FILE}"
    )
endif()
functions.description: Copy a sidecar DLL, SO, or dylib next to the executable according to the target platform.
functions.longDescription: When the same application is built on several desktop platforms, deploy the conventionally named sidecar shared library for the current target OS.

functions.name: CMake install runtime driver DLL
functions.description: Install a sidecar runtime DLL with the application package.
functions.longDescription: POST_BUILD copying is convenient for development. Add an install(FILES) rule as well when producing an installation tree or package.

functions.name: CMake deploy Qt runtime dependencies
functions.insertText:
include(GNUInstallDirs)

install(TARGETS MyQtApp
    BUNDLE DESTINATION .
    RUNTIME DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_BINDIR}
)

qt_generate_deploy_app_script(
    TARGET MyQtApp
    OUTPUT_SCRIPT deploy_script
    NO_UNSUPPORTED_PLATFORM_ERROR
)

install(SCRIPT ${deploy_script})
functions.description: Generate an install-time Qt deployment script for a self-contained desktop application directory.
functions.longDescription: Qt provides qt_generate_deploy_app_script() for install-time collection of the Qt runtime dependencies required by an application. It is available from Qt 6.3.

functions.name: CMake configure Qt prefix path Windows

functions.description: Configure a Qt project when CMake cannot find the selected Qt kit automatically.
functions.longDescription: CMAKE_PREFIX_PATH should point to the selected Qt installation prefix containing the Qt CMake package files. Use the directory matching your compiler kit, such as MinGW or MSVC.

functions.name: CMake configure build install workflow
functions.insertText:
cmake -S . -B build -G Ninja   -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release   -DCMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX="${PWD}/install"

cmake --build build --parallel
cmake --install build
functions.description: Configure, compile, and install a Qt application from the command line.
functions.longDescription: A separate build directory keeps generated files outside the sources. Running cmake --install executes the install rules and any generated Qt deployment script.

Previous workflow example retained from the original Qt QML pack:

```qml
Rectangle {
    width: 320
    height: 120
    color: slider.value > 50 ? "tomato" : "steelblue"

    Slider {
        id: slider
        anchors.centerIn: parent
        from: 0
        to: 100
        onValueChanged: console.log("value=" + value)
    }
}
```

```qml
MessageDialog {
```

```qml
    title: "Confirm"
    text: "Start the test sequence?"
    informativeText: "The relays will be commuted automatically."
```

items.description: Expose a Yes standard button in the QML dialog.

functions.longDescription:
Declare a Qt Quick Controls Dialog with standard buttons.

Previous workflow example retained from the original Qt QML pack:

```qml
import QtQuick
import QtQuick.Controls

Dialog {
    id: settingsDialog
    title: "Connection settings"
    modal: true
    standardButtons: Dialog.Ok | Dialog.Cancel

    onAccepted: backend.applySettings()
    onRejected: console.log("Canceled")
}

// settingsDialog.open()
```

Previous workflow example retained from the original Qt QML pack:

```qml
import QtQuick

Rectangle {
    id: root
    width: 320
    height: 120
    color: enabledCheck.checked ? "#16a34a" : "#991b1b"

    CheckBox {
        id: enabledCheck
        text: qsTr("Enabled")
    }
}
```
